strengths: 20+ years of engineering experience with demonstrated distributed-systems expertise; built high-scale microservices handling millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby
Full-stack technical depth across multiple languages and platforms; adaptable learner who has successfully contributed to diverse technical domains
Open-source leadership and community engagement showing communication skills, teaching ability, and capacity to mentor others
concerns: No demonstrated production experience building or operating multi-agent AI systems; recent MCP work at re:cinq (6 months) appears infrastructure/tooling-focused rather than architectural strategy
Minimal fintech infrastructure background; vague RIDE Capital exposure with no evidence of credit systems, regulatory compliance, payment flows, or trading systems expertise
Unclear track record scaling technical teams to 30+ people or developing senior technical leadership layers; no evidence of managing hybrid engineering or outsourced delivery partners

key_factors.factor: Fintech infrastructure and regulatory domain expertise
Distributed systems and backend architecture expertise

response.justification: Szabo has solid 20+ years of engineering experience with strong distributed-systems expertise, making him technically capable of infrastructure complexity. However, the CTO role requires demonstrated production experience with multi-agent AI systems and fintech infrastructure—both areas where he shows minimal background. His recent 6-month exposure to agentic AI tooling is insufficient for architecting complex agentic systems at the described scale. No clear evidence of scaling teams to 30+ people or operating in regulated environments. Better positioned as Principal Engineer or VP Engineering.
